methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $23M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Cover
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Cuby Technologies is a Series B-stage company in Housing & Construction that has raised $23.4M to date. The relevant comp universe is sparse: two private peers (ICON and Cover) operate in the sector, though neither provides a sufficient same-stage or valuation-tier matched set for reliable peer-median analysis.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable valuation lever here. Using total capital raised as a proxy base ($23.4M) and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B → subsequent-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $44M. This reflects normalized advance based on stage trajectory rather than revenue or margin metrics, which were not provided in the dataset.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $38M (low) to $51M (high), with a central estimate of $44M. The narrow range reflects reliance on a single methodological pillar; meaningful widening would require either (1) access to revenue, EBITDA, or unit economics for comparable Housing & Construction players, or (2) expansion of the peer set beyond the two identified private companies. Key uncertainty remains whether Cuby's capital efficiency and burn profile align with sector norms, which cannot be assessed from stage and raised capital alone. Any valuation refinement should prioritize gathering comparable transaction multiples or updated financials from ICON, Cover, or adjacent-sector Series B+ companies.
